---
title: "Interactive Data Table"
format: html
image: ucsb_campus.jpg
---

This interactive data table displays personal data collected over the span of a couple months. I wanted to see if daily workload impacted whether or not I went in the ocean, and what kind of ocean activity I did.  

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse) # data wrangling & viz (with {ggplot2})
library(plotly) # JS plots!
library(DT) # JS tables!
library(leaflet) # JS maps!


# reading in data ----
my_data <- read.csv("personal_data2.csv")
```

```{r}
#| echo: false

my_data_table <- datatable(data = my_data, 
                     # make the column names nice ----
                     colnames = c("Date", "Type of Ocean Activty", "Ocean", "Time of Activity", "Workload (hours)", "Weather", "Weekend/Weekday", "Ocean Conditions", "Mood"),
                     
                     # column filters: sliders and drop down menus ----
                     filter = "top", 
                     
                     # extensions: lots of these! ----
                     extensions = c("Buttons", "ColReorder"),
                     
                     # options ----
                     options = list(
                       # list 10 entries at once
                       pageLength = 10, 
                       # automatically size columns
                       autoWidth = TRUE,
                       # highlight entries that match search term
                       searchHighlight = TRUE,
                       # allow regular expressions and case insensitive searches
                       search = list(regex = TRUE, caseInsensitive = TRUE),
                       dom = "Bfrtip",
                       # buttons options
                       buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel", "pdf", "print", "colvis"),
                       # links to extension call
                       colReorder = TRUE)
) 
my_data_table
```
